explanation:
The **normal values in health** come from large population studies (NHANES, WHO, CDC). They vary by age, sex, ethnicity, and region. They are informational; your personal clinical history always takes priority.

## How it's calculated

Strategies to break through weight loss plateaus.

This calculator applies the formula with the values you enter, checking typical ranges and alerting for unreasonable values.

## Normal reference values

| Parameter | Normal adult range |
|---|---|
| Blood pressure | <120/80 mmHg |
| Resting heart rate | 60-100 bpm |
| Temperature | 36.1-37.2°C |
| SpO2 | 95-100% |
| Fasting glucose | 70-100 mg/dL |
| HbA1c | <5.7% |
| Total cholesterol | <200 mg/dL |
| LDL | <100 mg/dL |
| HDL | >40 (M) / >50 (F) |

## Final notes

This calculation is an informational reference. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult with a specialized professional. Values are updated for 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: What causes a weight loss plateau?
faq.a: After 2-3+ weeks of no weight loss, your body adapts to the calorie deficit. Reduced leptin levels, metabolic adaptation, and hormonal changes slow fat loss.

faq.q: How long does a weight loss plateau typically last?
faq.a: Most plateaus last 2-4 weeks. They're normal and don't mean your diet isn't working—your body is adjusting.

faq.q: How does a refeed day help break a plateau?
faq.a: Eating at maintenance calories for 1-2 weeks signals your body that the deficit isn't permanent, restoring leptin levels and restarting fat loss.

faq.q: Is a diet break the same as a refeed?
faq.a: Similar concept: both involve eating at maintenance calories for 1-2 weeks to reset hormones and allow fat loss to resume.

faq.q: Does cardio help break through a plateau?
faq.a: Increase daily activity (NEAT) and steps before jumping to intense cardio. Moderate activity works better than high-intensity exercise during plateaus.

faq.q: Can sleep affect my weight loss?
faq.a: Yes. Poor sleep (less than 7 hours) increases cortisol and hunger hormones, making plateaus worse. Prioritize 7+ hours nightly.

faq.q: How do I know if I'm actually at a plateau or just fluctuating?
faq.a: Track weight over 2-3 weeks. Daily fluctuations are normal due to water retention and digestion. A true plateau shows no downward trend over this period.

faq.q: When should I see a doctor about my weight loss plateau?
faq.a: Consult a healthcare provider if you have unusual symptoms, history of metabolic disorders, or your plateau persists beyond 4-6 weeks despite strategy changes.
